How I work

The Right solution for you

The best work I do starts with listening to the problem before offering a solution.

Before we talk about what might be the best solution for you, I want to understand your venue, your guests, and the challenges you're trying to solve. My role isn't to sell you a product, it's to help you find the right solution.

I approach every conversation as a consultant first and a salesperson second. That means asking better questions, challenging assumptions, and exploring opportunities you may not have considered. My goal is to help you make the best decision for your operation, not simply move a product.

The sales methodologies that shape my approach reflect that mindset. The Challenger Sale helps me uncover new possibilities. Solution-based selling keeps the focus on your unique needs. MEDDIC ensures every recommendation is tied to clear business outcomes and measurable value.

The result is a straightforward process, fewer surprises, and a trusted partner who is invested in getting it right, not just getting a signature.

Solution Based Selling

Fully electric. Self-contained. Ready to roll up anywhere.

The electric vehicles are a great example of how I sell. Clients rarely ask for an electric food service vehicle; they ask how to serve guests in areas where a traditional concession stand doesn't make sense.

Whether it's reaching fans in a remote section of a stadium, adding service across a sprawling campus, or creating a food and beverage presence without a costly construction project, the conversation starts with the challenge. The vehicle is simply one possible solution.

Because these units are fully self-contained, require no permanent infrastructure, and operate both indoors and outdoors, they give venues the flexibility to put service exactly where guests need it.

Footprint

Four configurations

One platform, built four ways.

Same chassis, same footprint, outfitted for whatever your venue actually serves. The honest part of my job is matching the right one to your problem, not upselling the one with the most equipment.

Godega

Your mobile convenience store
$85K–$90K

Hot food, fresh coffee, cold drinks, and snacks in one rolling store. The workhorse for everyday grab-and-go traffic.

The numbers

A Godega can pay for itself in about six months.

Modeled on a standard Godega running two sellouts a day at 75% sell-through. The point isn't the headline figure; it's that the math has to work for your venue before I recommend anything.

Revenue / day
$3,788
2 runs · 75% sellout
Revenue / month
$75,750
20 operating days
Revenue / year
$909K
240 operating days
Average net margin
20%
After cost of goods
Annual EBITDA
$181,800
Per vehicle
Time to payback
~180 days
Roughly a 6-month ROI

Illustrative model based on a standard FLA Godega at 2 runs/day, 75% sellout, 240 operating days. Actual results vary by venue, menu mix, pricing, and operating model. I'll build the numbers around your site, not the other way around.

The process

How a Project Actually Goes.

Here's how I take a project from our first conversation to a solution in service.

01

Discovery & Goals

We start with your operation, not a product. I work to understand your venue, guests, budget, stakeholders, and the challenges you're trying to solve. Whether the goal is increasing revenue, reducing lines, expanding service coverage, or activating unused space, we define success first.

02

Solution Development

Once we understand the problem, we explore the options. Sometimes the answer is obvious; sometimes it isn't. I evaluate your site, menu, operational requirements, and brand standards to determine what solution best fits your environment.

03

Design & Sales Engineering

After we've aligned on the direction, my team develops the vehicle configuration, layouts, renderings, and technical specifications. Every component is reviewed with you, along with transparent pricing and operational considerations.

04

Proposal & Refinement

We review the complete solution together, including design, pricing, timeline, and expected outcomes. I include up to two design revisions to ensure everything aligns with your vision before moving forward.

05

Agreement & Project Launch

Once the solution is finalized, we confirm scope, expectations, and project milestones. My goal is complete clarity on both sides before production begins.

06

Build, Delivery & Handoff

I transition the project to engineering and project management with a fully documented scope, locked timeline, and clear communication plan so you know exactly what to expect through delivery and launch.

Let's talk

Tell me what you're trying to solve.

No pitch decks until we know it's a fit. Tell me about your venue and the problem you're chasing, and I'll give you an honest read on whether this platform helps.
